# Gecko

> Construct network health monitoring

## About

| field | value |
|---|---|
| slug | `gecko` |
| version | `0.1.0` |
| schema_version | `3` |
| author | 0xHoneyJar |
| license | MIT |

Health monitoring for the construct ecosystem. Runs automated checks, detects when constructs drift from their documentation, and tracks lifecycle status. Four skills: patrol (automated loops), observe (single check), diagnose (deep investigation), report (findings summary).

## Skills

- `diagnose` — "Deep investigation of a single construct. Identity-reality drift, maintenance pattern, composition analysis."
- `observe` — "Single-pass network health observation. Checks API liveness, namespace freshness, drift, computes Network Health Score."
- `patrol` — "Autonomous observation loop. Time-boxed cycles with ratcheting health score, commits findings to grimoires."
- `report` — "Synthesize observations into a readable network health report. Aggregates patterns and trends from observation cycles."

## Commands

- `/diagnose` — Deep investigation of a single construct. Identity-reality drift, maintenance patterns,
- `/observe` — Single-pass network health observation. Checks API liveness, namespace freshness,
- `/patrol` — Autonomous observation loop. Time-boxed cycles with ratcheting health score.
- `/report` — Synthesize observations into a readable network health report.

```bash
/constructs install gecko
```
